title: Investigating biotin-BSM binding to streptavidin (that in turn is bound to dS-OEG-biotin) on QCM-D sensors
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1td7h","text":"This protocol is used for investigating biotin-BSM binding to streptavidin on QCM-D sensors.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare a Hepes Buffered Saline (HBS) solution containing 150 mM NaCl and 10 mM Hepes, a 100 µg/ml biotin-BSM solution in HBS and a 25 µg/ml streptavidin solution in HBS.
Make a baseline of BSM at a static QCM-D mode.
Flow the streptavidin solution over the modified sensors (100-500 µl/min), until they are saturated.
Wash the sensors by flowing them with HBS (100-500 μl/min).
Flow the sensors with the biotin-BSM solution (100-500 μl/min).
creator: Victoria Linderberg
